speaker: POMPEY
text: If the great gods be just, they shall assist

speaker: POMPEY
text: The deeds of justest men.

speaker: MENAS
text: Know, worthy Pompey,

speaker: MENAS
text: That what they do delay they not deny.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Whiles we are suitors to their throne, decays

speaker: POMPEY
text: The thing we sue for.

speaker: MENAS
text: We, ignorant of ourselves,

speaker: MENAS
text: Beg often our own harms, which the wise powers

speaker: MENAS
text: Deny us for our good; so find we profit

speaker: MENAS
text: By losing of our prayers.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I shall do well.

speaker: POMPEY
text: The people love me, and the sea is mine;

speaker: POMPEY
text: My powers are crescent, and my auguring hope

speaker: POMPEY
text: Says it will come to th’ full. Mark Antony

speaker: POMPEY
text: In Egypt sits at dinner, and will make

speaker: POMPEY
text: No wars without doors. Caesar gets money where

speaker: POMPEY
text: He loses hearts. Lepidus flatters both,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Of both is flattered; but he neither loves,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Nor either cares for him.

speaker: MENAS
text: Caesar and Lepidus

speaker: MENAS
text: Are in the field. A mighty strength they carry.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Where have you this? ’Tis false.

speaker: MENAS
text: From Silvius, sir.

speaker: POMPEY
text: He dreams. I know they are in Rome together,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Looking for Antony. But all the charms of love,

speaker: POMPEY
text: Salt Cleopatra, soften thy wanned lip!

speaker: POMPEY
text: Let witchcraft join with beauty, lust with both;

speaker: POMPEY
text: Tie up the libertine in a field of feasts;

speaker: POMPEY
text: Keep his brain fuming. Epicurean cooks

speaker: POMPEY
text: Sharpen with cloyless sauce his appetite,

speaker: POMPEY
text: That sleep and feeding may prorogue his honor

speaker: POMPEY
text: Even till a Lethe’d dullness—

speaker: POMPEY
text: How now, Varrius?

speaker: VARRIUS
text: This is most certain that I shall deliver:

speaker: VARRIUS
text: Mark Antony is every hour in Rome

speaker: VARRIUS
text: Expected. Since he went from Egypt ’tis

speaker: VARRIUS
text: A space for farther travel.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I could have given less matter

speaker: POMPEY
text: A better ear.—Menas, I did not think

speaker: POMPEY
text: This amorous surfeiter would have donned his helm

speaker: POMPEY
text: For such a petty war. His soldiership

speaker: POMPEY
text: Is twice the other twain. But let us rear

speaker: POMPEY
text: The higher our opinion, that our stirring

speaker: POMPEY
text: Can from the lap of Egypt’s widow pluck

speaker: POMPEY
text: The ne’er lust-wearied Antony.

speaker: MENAS
text: I cannot hope

speaker: MENAS
text: Caesar and Antony shall well greet together.

speaker: MENAS
text: His wife that’s dead did trespasses to Caesar;

speaker: MENAS
text: His brother warred upon him, although I think

speaker: MENAS
text: Not moved by Antony.

speaker: POMPEY
text: I know not, Menas,

speaker: POMPEY
text: How lesser enmities may give way to greater.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Were ’t not that we stand up against them all,

speaker: POMPEY
text: ’Twere pregnant they should square between themselves,

speaker: POMPEY
text: For they have entertainèd cause enough

speaker: POMPEY
text: To draw their swords. But how the fear of us

speaker: POMPEY
text: May cement their divisions and bind up

speaker: POMPEY
text: The petty difference, we yet not know.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Be ’t as our gods will have ’t. It only stands

speaker: POMPEY
text: Our lives upon to use our strongest hands.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Come, Menas.
